title: Witcher Medallions
description: Add functional Witcher Medallions to the Minecraft World.
body:
Dependencies:

Fabric:

NeoForge:

The witcher medallion is not only an insignia of our profession, it is also one of its tools. It has numerous useful capabilities that are accessible, of course, only to one who possesses the necessary knowledge and training.

- Vesemir, The World of the Witcher

Inspired by The Witcher series of books and games, this mod add functional Witcher Medallions to the world of Minecraft!

Features:

* Added 6 different medallions from each Witcher school plus an Ancient Wolf Medallion inspired by the design of the Netflix show, they all work exactly the same, they only differ aesthetically. * The medallion you have equipped will have a "soulbound" enchantment by default, so you will keep it even when you die. * The medallion has two ways of detecting monsters, passive and active. * Since 2.0.0, the medallion can also detect specific blocks with a strong passive detection. * Since 3.1.0 (Fabric), the medallions have full compatiblity with Witcher (More RPG Classes), adding attributes similar to the armors from that mod. >

The passive way:

>The medallion will vibrate when any enemies are nearby, and all of them (except the Ancient Wolf Medallion) will make animal sounds, each of their respective animal, this works even if you don't have the medallion equipped.

>

>
On inventory

>

>
Equipped

>

New in 2.0.0!

>

Strong detection:

>This is a special type of passive detection, it will detect magic blocks and strong magical mobs, the medallion will vibrate more aggressively and they will make different sounds than the normal passive detection.

>

>
The animation changing when detects a Warden.

>

>
The strong detection finding the End Portal.

>

The active way:

>Only if you have a medallion equipped, you can press a key (default T) to give to each nearby mob a client-side glowing effect. This has a small cooldown before you can press the key again.

>

>
Nearby mobs glowing.

>

>
The glow effect is selective; by default it will affect only "magical" mobs, such as undead mobs.

Crafting:

>

In 2.0.0 version!

>First of all, you will need to forge the deactivated medallion in a smithing table with a chain, an iron ingot (if you have a mod that adds silver, you can use a silver ingot instead) and a representative material from the Witcher School:

>

>
*For the Wolf School: Bone
>*For the Cat School: String or any fish
>*For the Bear School: Leather, Raw Salmon or Cooked Salmon
>*For the Griffin School: Feather
>*For the Viper School: Fermented Spider Eye
>*For the Manticore School: Raw Beef, Raw Porkchop, Raw Rabbit or Raw Mutton
>*For the Ancient Wolf Medallion: Paper

>You can now equip the deactivated medallion as a necklace, but for make it work you are going to need to activate it with a concentrated magical source; the Magic Nucleus:

>

>With this magical source in your hand, you can finally activate the medallion! You just need to use the smithing table and combine the Magic Nucleus with a medallion and a magic catalyst:

>

>As catalyst you can use any of the next items: >
*Gold Ingot
>*Lapis lazuli
>*Amethyst Shard
>*Echo Shard
>*Blaze Powder
>*Ghast Tear

Configuration:

>[Use Mod Menu for an easier configuration]

>MobList: Add any mob to make it detectable for the medallion, the syntax is "entity.[ModId].[EntityId]"

>StrongMagicSourcesList: Add any mob or block to make it detectable for strong detection, the syntax is "entity/block.[ModId].[Entity/BlockId]"

>activeDetectionSize: The size in blocks of the area in which the medallion can detect mobs when the respective key is pressed (Default: 35)

>passiveDetectionXZ: The size (X and Z) in blocks in which the medallion can detect mobs with the animal sounds and vibration animation (Default: 15)

>passiveDetectionY: The height on blocks below and above of you at which the medallion can detect mobs with the animal sounds and vibration animation. Remember, if you set a very high value here it will detect mobs in caves below you (Default: 4)

>StrongpassiveDetectionXZ: The size (X and Z) in blocks at which the medallion can detect mobs or blocks with strong magical auras (Default: 20)

>StrongpassiveDetectionY: The height on blocks below and above of you at which the medallion can detect mobs or blocks with strong magical auras. Remember, if you set a very high value here it will detect mobs in caves below you (Default: 8)

>StrongDetectionForBlocks: Activates or deactivates the blocks detection (Default: true)

>medallionSounds: Controls whether the medallion makes animal sounds in the passive detection (Default: true)

>medallionsHaveSoulbound: Controls whether the equipped medallion is retained after death (Default: true)

Credits and Recommended mods:

* Thanks to Mlodziak00 for helping me with the Trinkets+Geckolib integration! Check the Familiarity mod! (Currently available only for 1.19.2)

* Take a look at A Witcher - An Origins Addon my principal inspiration and the reason this mod even exist! Seriously, check it out, it's a really amazing mod. (Currently available only for 1.19.2)

* If you want adrenaline and signs check Witcher (More RPG Classes)! Fully supported!

* If you want new Witcher mobs, armors and weapons check my other mod The Conjunction of the Spheres

* And of course thanks to Andrzej Sapkowski and CD Projekt RED for creating this amazing universe!

Disclaimer: Sounds are modified versions of sounds from Zapsplat.com, Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings, Witcher 3: Wild Hunt and Minecraft itself.
